suggested_cluster_label: Thin-film solid-state battery stack fabrication via sputter deposition
engineering_module_candidates: Multilayer stack patterning and integration on substrate
Sputter/plasma deposition process and target system
Energy storage cell assembly and current collector formation
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms (electrode, electrolyte, cathode, substrate, layer, cell, plasma, deposition, target, battery, electrochemical, energy storage) combined with titles like 'ENERGY STORAGE DEVICE', 'STACK FOR AN ENERGY STORAGE DEVICE', and 'METHOD OF MAKING A COMPONENT FOR A SOLID-STATE ELECTROCHEMICAL CELL' indicate the cluster centers on solid-state thin-film batteries. Abstracts repeatedly describe depositing first electrode, electrolyte, and second electrode layers on a substrate, with grooves and current collectors, supporting modules for layer deposition, stack patterning, and sputter/plasma processing.

suggested_cluster_label: Haircare appliance with airflow-based styling attachments
engineering_module_candidates: Airflow generation subsystem (air inlet, air outlet, airflow generator)
Interchangeable styling attachments (e.g., diffuser, handheld attachments)
Attachment airflow-shaping geometry (curved/flat surfaces downstream of outlet)
rationale: Top terms ('haircare', 'haircare appliance', 'hair styling', 'airflow', 'air inlet', 'air outlet', 'attachment', 'diffuser') and representative titles ('HAIRCARE APPLIANCE') consistently point to a hair styling appliance built around an airflow generator. Abstracts explicitly describe air inlet/outlet configurations, attachments, and curved/flat surfaces adjacent to the air outlet, supporting modules for airflow generation, attachments, and outlet-shaping geometry.

suggested_cluster_label: Handheld fluid-flow appliances (hairdryer-type) with internal duct/flow-path architecture
engineering_module_candidates: Internal duct and fluid flow path
rationale: Top terms ('fluid', 'fluid flow', 'flow path', 'fluid inlet', 'fluid outlet', 'hand held appliance', 'wall', 'duct') and titles repeatedly featuring 'HAND HELD APPLIANCE', 'attachment hand', 'cleaning appliance', and 'heater assembly' point to handheld appliances (explicitly exemplified as hairdryers in abstracts) defined by an internal fluid flow path from inlet to outlet through a duct within a body. Abstracts confirm body, duct, primary fluid flow path, inlet, and outlet as recurring sub-systems, with title terms supporting heater and attachment modules. Cleaning-appliance references suggest the cluster may also span other handheld fluid-flow appliances, lowering confidence that it is hairdryer-exclusive.

suggested_cluster_label: Sensor-driven smart vacuum cleaners and mobile cleaning robots
engineering_module_candidates: Vacuum motor and suction control subsystem
rationale: Top terms ('vacuum motor', 'vacuum cleaner', 'sensor', 'controller', 'mobile robot') and repeated 'VACUUM CLEANER' titles dominate the cluster. Abstracts consistently describe a vacuum motor paired with motion/orientation sensors and a controller that processes sensor signals (including via a 'surface type model') to determine surface type, plus a cleaner head with agitator and diagnostic sensors. The presence of 'mobile', 'mobile robot', and 'robotic' in title terms supports a robotic/mobile-cleaning sub-theme. Mentions of 'oral' and 'hairstyling' in title terms appear but are not reflected in the provided abstracts, so those sub-themes are weak/ambiguous and excluded from module candidates with lower confidence.

suggested_cluster_label: Vacuum Cleaner Head with Agitator/Brush Bar Assembly
engineering_module_candidates: Cleaner head housing and suction chamber
Agitator / brush bar rotation and drive mechanism
Suction inlet and airflow path to suction generator
Debris illumination module (e.g., laser/LED for surface visibility)
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('cleaner head', 'agitator', 'brush bar', 'suction', 'housing', 'chamber') and repeated titles 'CLEANER HEAD FOR A VACUUM CLEANER' clearly define this cluster as vacuum cleaner head designs. Abstracts explicitly describe a housing defining a suction chamber with inlet/outlet, single or dual agitators mounted for rotation, and drive mechanisms—supporting the housing, agitator, and airflow modules. One abstract mentions a laser diode for green-light debris illumination, supporting an illumination sub-module, though this appears in fewer examples so its prominence is lower.

suggested_cluster_label: Surface treating appliance main body and support/wand architecture
rationale: Top terms 'main body', 'surface treating appliance', 'support assembly', 'hose', 'wand', and 'surface treating head' along with abstracts describing a vacuum cleaner main body with a rollable support assembly, hose/wand assembly, and surface treating head directly support these modules. Titles emphasize 'surface treating appliance' and 'wand assembly', confirming the architectural focus on the overall appliance body and its mobility/wand subsystems rather than internal components like cyclones or filters.

suggested_cluster_label: Multi-stage cyclonic dirt/dust separation for vacuum cleaners
engineering_module_candidates: Dust collection chamber (e.g., annular dirt bin)
Airflow path / inlet-outlet ducting for separator assembly
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('cyclonic', 'separating', 'cyclone', 'dirt', 'dust', 'separating unit', 'chamber', 'stage') and titles like 'MULTISTAGE CYCLONIC SEPARATING APPARATUS' and 'Dirt and Dust Cyclonic Separating Apparatus' clearly indicate cyclonic separation technology. Abstracts explicitly describe a first cyclonic unit with cyclone(s) and an annular dust collecting chamber plus a downstream second cyclonic unit, supporting multi-stage cyclone and dust chamber modules. References to 'handheld cleaning appliance' with dirty air inlet, clean air outlet, and separating apparatus support the airflow path and handheld vacuum integration modules.

engineering_module_candidates: Nozzle with interior passage and air-emitting mouth
rationale: Top terms ('nozzle', 'interior passage', 'mouth', 'emitted', 'impeller', 'air outlet', 'fan assembly') and abstracts describing a 'bladeless fan assembly' with 'a nozzle mounted on a base' containing an 'interior passage and a mouth' through which air flow is emitted directly support a nozzle-driven bladeless fan architecture with a base housing the impeller/motor. The title term 'humidifying' and 'nozzle fan' suggest a humidifier variant as an additional module.

suggested_cluster_label: Brushless (permanent-magnet) motor control via winding excitation
engineering_module_candidates: AC-to-rectified-voltage input stage for motor drive
rationale: Titles are dominated by 'CONTROL OF A BRUSHLESS MOTOR' and 'CONTROL OF A BRUSHLESS PERMANENT-MAGNET MOTOR', and top terms (winding, voltage, brushless, phase winding, current, period) align with the abstracts, which consistently describe exciting a motor winding with a (rectified) voltage, freewheeling when winding current exceeds a threshold, and using timed overrun/excitation periods. This points to control electronics for brushless PM motors, including a rectifier front-end and current/time-based winding excitation control.

engineering_module_candidates: Rotor assembly (shaft, rotor core, magnet, impeller)
Stator assembly (stator core, C-shaped core variants)
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms (rotor, stator, bearing, shaft, frame, stator core, impeller, adhesive) and title terms (brushless motor, compressor, turbomachine, rotor assembly) consistently point to brushless motor construction. Representative abstracts explicitly describe a rotor assembly with shaft/impeller/bearing/rotor core, a stator assembly, and an enclosing frame, with one variant noting a C-shaped stator core and another integrating a heat sink into a compressor — supporting the listed sub-system modules.

suggested_cluster_label: Cleaning appliances (dental fluid-jet and floor/vacuum tools)
engineering_module_candidates: Floor tool head for vacuum cleaning appliance
rationale: Top terms ('cleaning appliance', 'floor', 'floor tool', 'vacuum cleaning appliance', 'working fluid', 'reservoir', 'conduit', 'suction', 'handle', 'dental') and abstracts describing dental cleaning appliances with handle, detachable cleaning tool, nozzle, fluid reservoir and moveable fluid conduit indicate two related sub-themes: dental fluid-burst cleaners and floor/vacuum cleaning tools. Module candidates map directly to parts named in the abstracts (handle, cleaning tool, nozzle, reservoir, conduit) and to title terms ('floor tool', 'vacuum cleaning'). The mixed dental + floor/vacuum evidence lowers confidence that this is a single coherent product; it is better viewed as a broader 'cleaning appliance' cluster.

suggested_cluster_label: Wearable air purification and air-treatment appliances
engineering_module_candidates: Filter assembly (air filtration media and housing)
Motor-driven impeller / fan assembly for airflow generation
Wearable ear/head-mounted form factor with integrated speaker assembly
Airflow path and delivery ducting downstream of filter
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms (filter, air, airflow, fan, wearable, filter assembly, purifier, air purifier) and title terms (wearable air, air purifier, fan assembly, filter) point to air purification devices. Representative titles ('HEAD WEARABLE AIR PURIFIER', 'WEARABLE AIR PURIFIER', 'AIR TREATMENT APPARATUS') and abstracts repeatedly describe a filter assembly, a motor-driven impeller creating airflow through the filter, and ear/speaker assemblies worn over the user's ears, supporting the wearable purifier theme and the listed modules. Terms like 'dryer', 'hand dryer', and 'drying' also appear, suggesting some adjacent air-handling appliances are mixed in, which slightly lowers confidence that the cluster is purely wearable purifiers.
